page: Amazon Web Services m5d.24xlarge is a general-purpose, Nitro-hypervised instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon 8259CL cores operating at 3.1 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports AVX and AVX-512 instructions. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 3600 GB of NVMe SSD instance storage, complemented by a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. No GPU accelerators are present on this hardware profile.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and high passmark CPU scores, alongside strong memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached read operations. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput are also highly rated. However, single-core performance is relatively weak, as evidenced by lower stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores. Additionally, while LLM prompt processing is average, text generation speeds for small and medium models are poor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5d.24xlarge offers high resource density by combining substantial compute and memory capacities with large, high-speed local NVMe storage on a single instance. This eliminates the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput local operations. The hardware profile is highly suitable for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, static web serving, and memory-intensive general-purpose applications, though it is less optimal for single-threaded tasks or generative AI inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. It features 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 3.1 GHz, 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal strong multi-core CPU execution, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database and Redis SET throughput. However, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are weak. Qualitatively, the instance provides high resource density by bundling massive compute and memory with local NVMe storage, making it cost-effective for high-throughput local data processing. It is best suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and static web serving, whereas single-threaded tasks and generative AI workloads are less optimal.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.24xlarge is a 96-vCPU general-purpose instance with 384 GB RAM and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A high-density general-purpose platform combining 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 3.1 GHz
384.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and static web serving performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Weak single-core performance and poor LLM text generation speeds
Well-suited for multi-threaded databases and high-throughput local processing
